name: Couscous
description: Fluffy couscous in about 15 minutes — toasted in olive oil, then steamed off the heat in hot water or broth.
recipeIngredient: Optional: chopped parsley, mint, or a squeeze of lemon
recipeInstructions: Set a medium pot over medium heat and add the olive oil. When it shimmers, add the couscous and toast, stirring, until lightly golden and fragrant, 2 to 3 minutes.
Carefully add the hot water or broth and the salt, give it one quick stir, and immediately turn off the heat.
Cover and let sit undisturbed for about 10 minutes, until the liquid is fully absorbed.
Fluff with a fork and stir in fresh herbs or a squeeze of lemon if using.
author: The Mediterranean Dish

ai.cliffNotes: Toast the couscous in oil for nuttiness, then let it steam off the heat so the grains stay separate and fluffy.
keyTips: Use a 1.5:1 liquid-to-couscous ratio and let it sit covered off the heat — boiling it makes it gummy.
Swap water for broth and finish with herbs or lemon to make it taste like more than a side.
